description: Duplicate workflow button + default Google Service Account JSON + mark existing rows as processed on new/changed sheet
steps: Step 1: Add a 'Duplicate' button to each workflow card in src/dashboard/views/workflows.ts. The button should call a JS function duplicateWorkflow(id) that fetches the workflow via GET /api/workflows/:id, pre-fills the new workflow form with all values from the source workflow (name appended with ' (copy)', same sheetId, worksheetName, bufferAccessToken, bufferChannelId, pollingInterval), and loads the credentials JSON from the source workflow's credentials file for the textarea. The form-workflow-id should be empty so it submits as a new POST.

Step 2: Add a POST /api/workflows/:id/duplicate endpoint in src/dashboard/dashboard-routes.ts that reads the existing workflow's full config (including credentials JSON from the file on disk), creates a new workflow with '(copy)' suffix on name, and returns the new workflow. This is the server-side approach if needed, but the client-side approach (loading into the form) is preferred since the user may want to edit before saving.

Step 3: For duplicate via the form (client-side), add a new API endpoint GET /api/workflows/:id/credentials that returns the raw credentials JSON for a workflow (reading from the file path in workflow config). This allows the duplicate function to populate the credentials textarea. Add this endpoint in dashboard-routes.ts, protected by requireAuth.

Step 4: Hard-code the default Google Service Account JSON in src/dashboard/views/workflows.ts. When the 'New Workflow' form is shown (showNewForm function), pre-fill the wf-credentials textarea with the following JSON:

This should also be the default when duplicating (unless the source workflow has different credentials).

Step 5: Mark existing rows as processed when a new workflow is created or an existing workflow's sheetId is changed. In src/workflows/workflow-manager.ts, in the addWorkflow method, after saving the workflow and before starting it, fetch all current rows from the sheet and add their row numbers to the processedRows set. Similarly in updateWorkflow, if sheetId changed, reset the processedRows for that workflow with all current rows from the new sheet. This ensures only rows added AFTER save time get published. The implementation should: authenticate the poller, call a new method like fetchAllRowNumbers() on SheetPoller that returns all row numbers (not just unprocessed ones), then save those to the processed-rows file.

Step 6: Add a fetchAllRowNumbers() method to SheetPoller in src/poller/sheet-poller.ts that authenticates and returns all row numbers currently in the sheet, regardless of processed state. This is used by WorkflowManager to pre-mark existing rows.
acceptance_criteria: Each workflow card has a 'Duplicate' button that opens the form pre-filled with all config from the source workflow
The New Workflow form pre-fills the Google Service Account JSON textarea with the hardcoded default credentials
When duplicating, credentials are loaded from the source workflow's file and shown in the textarea
When a new workflow is created, all existing rows in the sheet are marked as processed before polling starts
When an existing workflow's sheetId is updated, the processed rows are reset to all current rows in the new sheet
findings: All changes compile clean and all 117 tests pass. Added fetchAllRowNumbers() to ISheetPoller interface which required updating the mock in automation-service.test.ts. The credentials endpoint reads from disk using the workflow's googleCredentialsPath. Default credentials are embedded as a JSON.stringify call in client-side JS.
